response.justification: The candidate brings genuine distributed-systems depth from Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O, microservices), active hands-on AI-agent and MCP work in the current re:cinq role, and strong TypeScript across multiple recent positions. However, Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being listed as a must-have, which is the most significant disqualifying gap. Staff-level technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder management are also not clearly evidenced — the profile reads more as a strong senior IC than a domain-level technical strategist. Worth a brief screening call specifically to probe Python proficiency and strategic scope before dismissing.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed systems experience: millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers, caching, and low-latency data pipelines
Directly on-target AI-native exposure in the current role — contributing to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
Strong TypeScript proficiency across multiple recent production roles, plus broad platform breadth (embedded, mobile, web, DevOps) that supports systems-thinking requirements
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and every role description, yet the job lists it as a must-have technical skill — this is the single largest gap and could be disqualifying on its own

Evidence of Staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, and domain-level decision-making is thin; the resume reads as a prolific IC rather than someone who has driven technical direction at scale
No explicit mention of operational ownership, on-call rotation, or incident-response experience, all of which are listed as must-have non-technical requirements
